business.headline: Sensex falls 800 points: Why is stock market down today?Markets opened sharply lower as renewed Iran-US tensions, higher crude prices and heavy IT profit-booking hit trade on Wednesday. The weaker rupee, persistent FPI outflow concerns and the RBI policy outlook kept volatility elevated.
Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade over crude oil price rise; TCS falls 6%Sensex falls 600 points, Nifty below 23,400; TCS drops 4%

IndusInd Bank faces fresh whistleblower allegations; complaint sent to PMO, RBIA whistleblower complaint has sought a probe into alleged insider trading and governance lapses at IndusInd Bank. The allegations add fresh scrutiny as the lender remains under watch over its Rs 2,000-crore derivatives accounting discrepancy.

Not just the AI king. Nvidia may be the world's safest companyNvidia's credit default swap has traded close to US sovereign levels as its market value and earnings surge. The reading reflects unusually strong confidence in the AI chipmaker's ability to withstand shocks and meet obligations.

US wants to end Russian oil sanctions waiver: How it will hit IndiaThe US has repeatedly criticised purchases of Russian oil since the Ukraine conflict began, claiming that revenues from crude exports help fund Moscow's military campaign.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Oyo parent Prism gets Sebi nod for Rs 6,650 crore IPO, targets up to $8 billion valuationFollowing Sebi's approval, the company is now expected to file an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-1), which will be made available for public review and comments.

Waiting for Form 16? Here's when you'll receive it and why it matters for your ITRThe countdown to ITR filing has begun, and Form 16 remains one of the most important documents for salaried taxpayers. Knowing when your employer must issue it and what information it contains can help you prepare for a smoother tax filing experience.

Sensex ends 382 points higher, Nifty nears 23,500 led by IT sector rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 382.50 points, or 0.52%, to close at 74,649.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 100.95 points, or 0.43%, to settle at 23,483.55.

From 5th to 7th: Why India is losing the stock market race against Asian rivalsIndia has slipped from fifth to seventh in global stock market rankings as South Korea and Taiwan moved ahead. The shift shows investors are favouring AI-linked semiconductor hubs over markets with limited listed chipmakers.

8th Pay Commission: Final date to submit salary and pension demands extendedAnother extension, more questions. The 8th Pay Commission has pushed back its submission deadline again, keeping employees guessing about pay hikes and arrears.

Will your home loan EMI rise again? What RBI's June MPC meet could mean for borrowersIf you have a home loan, the RBI's June policy meeting could be worth watching closely. Borrowers are eager to know whether their EMIs will remain stable or face another change in the months ahead.

Why is Nvidia hiring when the rest of Silicon Valley is firingNvidia is expanding H-1B hiring even as major technology rivals cut jobs and slow foreign recruitment. The move stands out for Indian professionals facing layoffs, visa pressure and a tighter US market.

Why your society maintenance bill could soon be Rs 3,000 higher every monthFor many apartment residents, the next surprise could arrive not in their power bill or fuel bill, but in their society maintenance statement. Rising wage costs and higher operating expenses are forcing several housing societies to consider significant fee hikes.

India-US trade talks begin today: Why Section 301 could become the biggest issueIndian and US negotiators have begun fresh trade talks in New Delhi with Section 301 emerging as a central issue. India wants assurance that future US tariff action will not erode gains from a bilateral trade deal.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech jump up to 6%. 3 reasons why IT stocks are rallying today?IT stocks extended gains for a third straight session, led by Infosys, TCS, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra. The rally reflects stronger global tech sentiment, AI spending optimism, rate-cut hopes and support from a weaker rupee.

After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India in global stock market rankingsSouth Korea has moved past India in global stock market rankings after a sharp rally in AI-linked chipmakers. The shift underlines how investors are favouring markets tied to the AI supply chain while Indian equities face near-term pressures.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,300; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 302.16 points, or 0.41%, to 73,965.18 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 107.05 points, or 0.46%, to 23,275.55 as of 9:25 am.

Trump cuts tariffs on farm, industrial equipment from 25% to 15%. What it meansUS President Donald Trump has lowered US tariffs on selected farm and industrial equipment from 25% to 15% through December 2027. The move couples cheaper imports with incentives for greater use of American steel and aluminium.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

entertainment.headline: Peddi box office prediction: Rs 100 crore opening and Ram Charan's game changing bet Trade analysts expect Peddi to open strongly, driven by the Telugu market and overseas sales. The film's hold beyond day one could determine Ram Charan's standing as a solo box-office draw.

Rhea Chakraborty says Aryan Khan's arrest echoed her brother Showik's ordeal Rhea Chakraborty said Aryan Khan's 2021 drug case looked strikingly similar to what her brother Showik faced. She also spoke about the emotional strain of jail, public scrutiny and the friends who stood by her family.

How Inde Navarrette's performance changed Nikki's ending in Obsession Curry Barker said Obsession originally gave Nikki a much darker ending before Inde Navarrette's performance changed the plan. The decision followed a single survival-scene take that left the set silent and reshaped the character's fate.

Ranveer Singh sues film body a week after 'ban' amid Don 3 row Ranveer Singh has sent a legal notice to FWICE after its non-cooperation directive over Don 3. The move sharpens the dispute involving Excel Entertainment as the film body prepares another press conference.

Obsession has a Bart Simpson link, it's not the only reason Gen Z loves the film Director Curry Barker has revealed how an episode of The Simpsons inspired his global hit, Obsession. It connects with Gen Z thought process, though there is more.

Obsession's One Wish Willow shop is real, and open to visitors. Here's where it is One of the most intriguing details about Obsession lies beyond the film itself. The occult shop where the story begins is a real place in California, while the mysterious One Wish Willow that drives the plot has also been turned into a product fans can actually buy.

Ram Charan's Peddi is Tollywood's RCB moment: An industry desperate for June win Ram Charan's Peddi has emerged as a crucial release for Telugu cinema after the industry's a weak theatrical run in 2026. Its performance will shape confidence around star-led films, theatres and the industry's broader recovery.

Are Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein a couple? Singer-actor breaks silence Ever since singer-actor Jennifer Lopez and actor Brett Goldstein began promoting their film Office Romance, questions about their off-screen relationship have followed them everywhere. During a recent interview, the pair finally tackled the rumours head-on.

Peddi advance booking: Ram Charan's film needs a late push, trails Game Changer Ram Charan's sports action drama Peddi is generating strong advance booking numbers in India and overseas, signalling a promising opening. The film's global appeal, especially in North America, is boosting its pre-release momentum ahead of the June 4 release.

Obsession box office: Curry Barker's horror sensation earns Rs 12 crore in India Debutant Curry Barker's low-budget horror film Obsession is steadily gaining attention in India. Backed by strong word-of-mouth, praise from Bollywood actors and a solid box office run, the film has emerged as a talking point among moviegoers.

Actors' body reacts to non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh in Don 3 row Actors' body CINTAA has expressed concern over the handling of the Ranveer Singh-FWICE dispute, stating that it was not consulted before the issue spilled into the public domain. Members also underscored the need for dialogue and a proper dispute-resolution mechanism within the film industry.

Shilpa Shinde says her sexual harassment claims against Bhabiji producer were false Actor Shilpa Shinde has made a revelation regarding her past legal dispute linked to Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain!. She said that the sexual harassment allegations she made against producer Sanjay Kohli were false and were part of a larger conflict at the time.

Badshah's Chandigarh nightclub sealed over building violations Singer-rapper Badshah's nightclub SAGO in Chandigarh's Sector 26 has been sealed by the administration over alleged unauthorised constructions and building norm violations.

I shouldn't have laughed: Pranit More apologises over 'peak Gurgaon content' clip Pranit More apologised after a stand-up clip showed him laughing at an audience member's remarks about "recovering" the cost of a date, triggering online backlash.

Dua Lipa, Callum Turner make it official in wedding pics that scream peak romance Dua Lipa and Callum Turner made their wedding official with dreamy photos on social media. The singer shared glimpses from their intimate London ceremony, offering fans the first official look at her special day.

Kangana's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata trailer packs terror and survival in 26/11 story The trailer of Bharat Bhagya Vidhata, starring Kangana Ranaut, offers a glimpse into the courage of the nurses who protected patients during the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.
Success brings enemies: Kangana Ranaut backs Ranveer Singh amid Don 3 ban row Kangana Ranaut backed Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 ban row. She said success often brings enemies and obstacles.

Euphoria 3 finale: Not just Rue, the series failed all its women As Euphoria reached its final stretch, its women increasingly became symbols rather than people. That shift left the series facing questions about whether it examined female experience or merely packaged it as spectacle.

Ram Charan's bodyguard tackles fan breaching security, Janhvi Kapoor left stunned A fan dressed like Ram Charan rushed towards the stage at the Peddi pre-release event in Vijayawada before being stopped by the actor's personal security guard. Janhvi Kapoor was left shocked as the fan almost fell on her, while running to see Charan.

general news.headline: US proposes up to 12.5% tariffs on India, China under Section 301 actionThe United States has proposed new Section 301 tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from India, China and 58 other economies over forced labour concerns. The move, still under review, signals a wider push to tie trade enforcement to labour standards and supply chain scrutiny.

Iran attacks US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain in retaliation for Qeshm Island strikesIran's armed forces said they targeted the US Fifth Fleet and a US air base in the region in retaliation for alleged US attacks on an Iranian telecommunications antenna and oil tanker, adding that they had carried out a previously warned "more severe" response.

Ranveer Singh sues film body a week after 'ban' amid Don 3 rowRanveer Singh has sent a legal notice to FWICE after its non-cooperation directive over Don 3. The move sharpens the dispute involving Excel Entertainment as the film body prepares another press conference.

Praggnanandhaa beats Carlsen twice, matching Vishy Anand's 19-year-old featHistory repeats itself as Indian GM Praggnanandhaa defeats World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen twice in a single classical tournament, joining Viswanathan Anand in one of chess's most exclusive clubs.

Pakistanis build mosque illegally in Japan. Embarrassments followThe mosque in Japan's Kawagoe city was inaugurated by Pakistan's Ambassador to Japan, Abdul Hameed, in April. Weeks later, the Pakistani mission was forced to issue a clarification after it emerged that the structure had been built without the necessary approvals from local authorities. Locals are now demanding the structure be razed.

Maharashtra govt buys Mumbai's landmark Air India building for Rs 1,601 croreThe state government plans to shift a number of its offices to the Air India building, many of which are currently functioning from rented premises across Mumbai.

Girls locked in cages, raped by 700 men: UK MP shares Pak grooming gang testimoniesBritish MP Rupert Lowe read out harrowing accounts of systematic sexual assault, violence, racial targeting, intimidation and alleged police misconduct, alongside allegations that public authorities, healthcare workers and children's home staff repeatedly failed to protect vulnerable children.

Resign, Balen: Nepal students, Opposition protest against PM's India remarkPrime Minister Balen Shah's remark that Nepal too might have crossed into Indian territory has triggered a political firestorm, seemingly uniting a fractured opposition, now asking for his resignation. Balen, who rode a wave of protests to become the PM, is himself facing huge protests.

Army pilot proposes to girlfriend in front of helicopter during convocation in UPAn Army pilot proposed to his girlfriend in front of a helicopter right after graduating from CAATS in Nashik. The surprise moment drew cheers at the site and went viral on social media.

UPSC has built a leakproof island. Here's how it prevents paper leaksThe Supreme Court, while slamming the NTA over the NEET paper leak, praised the UPSC for its ability to conduct large-scale examinations without scandals. What exactly does the UPSC do differently? What do the experts say? Does that mean the UPSC model is foolproof?

Siddaramaiah's son may take oath today, Parameshwara likely Deputy CMKarnataka chief minister-designate DK Shivakumar will take oath on June 3 along with a 13-member cabinet, with Siddaramaiah's son Yathindra among those set to be inducted, while G Parameshwara is likely to be the sole deputy chief minister, sources said.

Satya Nadella says days of operating system and apps are over, era of Agents is beginningMicrosoft CEO Satya Nadella says computing is entering a new era where AI agents, not operating systems or apps, become the primary interface. The company unveiled Project Solara, a platform designed with Qualcomm to power agent-first devices capable of working across apps, workflows and screens.

Teen flags JEE Advanced data exposure of over 3 lakh records, IIT Roorkee respondsA 16-year-old cybersecurity researcher alleged that a cloud storage misconfiguration linked to JEE Advanced 2026 made result records and admit-card PDFs publicly accessible. IIT Roorkee said corrective action was underway, but the disclosure has renewed concerns over student data security.

Shilpa Shinde says her sexual harassment claims against Bhabiji producer were falseActor Shilpa Shinde has made a revelation regarding her past legal dispute linked to Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain!. She said that the sexual harassment allegations she made against producer Sanjay Kohli were false and were part of a larger conflict at the time.

DK Shivakumar to be sworn in as Karnataka CM, 13 MLAs likely to take oathA long political journey reaches its defining moment as DK Shivakumar prepares to take oath as Karnataka Chief Minister, opening a new chapter in the state's politics after Siddaramaiah's exit.

Actors' body reacts to non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh in Don 3 rowActors' body CINTAA has expressed concern over the handling of the Ranveer Singh-FWICE dispute, stating that it was not consulted before the issue spilled into the public domain. Members also underscored the need for dialogue and a proper dispute-resolution mechanism within the film industry.

Obsession's One Wish Willow shop is real, and open to visitors. Here's where it isOne of the most intriguing details about Obsession lies beyond the film itself. The occult shop where the story begins is a real place in California, while the mysterious One Wish Willow that drives the plot has also been turned into a product fans can actually buy.

Termite-damaged cash worth Rs 1 crore found in Kolkata college roomBundles of termite-damaged currency notes, reportedly including Rs 100 and Rs 500 notes, were recovered from two suitcases found inside a college union room at Surendranath College during a cleanliness drive in Kolkata.

Twisha's mother-in-law Giribala Singh jailed, now identified as prisoner number 71Giribala Singh and her son, Samarth, were sent to Bhopal Central Jail on Tuesday. According to jail sources, the former judge has been housed in the women's medical ward and assigned prisoner number 71.

Unpaid salaries, bounced cheques: 700 lose jobs as Pune IT firm abruptly shuts opsThe company initially built trust through timely salaries and intern stipends, but employees say payments began getting delayed earlier this year, with salaries due since February remaining unpaid.

Ram Charan's Peddi is Tollywood's RCB moment: An industry desperate for June winRam Charan's Peddi has emerged as a crucial release for Telugu cinema after the industry's a weak theatrical run in 2026. Its performance will shape confidence around star-led films, theatres and the industry's broader recovery.
Why is monsoon arrival in India delayed? Science behind it explainedThe southwest monsoon is late beyond its expected onset over Kerala. Here's why the Indian Ocean is delaying the monsoon's arrival.

Annamalai tells BJP leaders he will resign, party asks him to wait: SourcesDespite the growing speculation, BJP leaders remain hopeful that Annamalai can be persuaded to stay. Party insiders acknowledge that his departure would be a major setback for the BJP's long-term ambitions in Tamil Nadu, where he is widely credited with energising the cadre base and giving the party unprecedented visibility.

Lokhande Sitaram named CBSE chief, Varun Bhardwaj Secretary after OSM rowWhat began as complaints over CBSE's on-screen marking system has now led to an inquiry, a leadership shake-up and a political battle over who should be held accountable.

sports.headline: Fonseca far from reaching limits as dream run ends in French Open quarterfinals Jakub Mensik beat Joao Fonseca in straight sets to reach his first Grand Slam semi-final at Roland Garros. Fonseca said the defeat still strengthened his belief in his game, body and mentality.

Praggnanandhaa beats Carlsen twice, matching Vishy Anand's 19-year-old feat History repeats itself as Indian GM Praggnanandhaa defeats World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen twice in a single classical tournament, joining Viswanathan Anand in one of chess's most exclusive clubs.

Zverev overcomes teen scare to enter French Open semi-finals French Open: Second seed Alexander Zverev stormed into the semi-finals of Roland Garros after beating talented 19-year-old Rafael Jodar. The Spaniard gave Zverev a run for his money in the opening set but faded out later.

Suryakumar Yadav's poor run continues, fails in Mumbai T20 League after IPL India captain Suryakumar Yadav's struggles continued outside the Indian Premier League after the batter failed to assert his dominance in the city-based Mumbai T20 league. Chasing 210 runs vs Ajinkya Rahane's North Mumbai Panthers, Suryakumar got out for just 19 runs.

Sachin Tendulkar analyses IPL 2026 in detail, advocates new powerplay rule Legendary Sachin Tendulkar has analysed the Indian Premier League's 2026 season in detail. Taking to Reddit, Sachin has opened up on the experience of watching Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the distinct identities the teams have taken, and has even advocated a new powerplay rule.

Kostyuk dedicates maiden Grand Slam semi-final to difficult nights in Ukraine Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk gave an emotional speech after reaching her first Grand Slam semi-final. Kostyuk beat her compatriot Elina Svitolina in a 3-set battle to reach the semis of the French Open.

Lakshya Sen knocked out in first round, Kidambi Srikanth's horror run continues PV Sindhu opened with a straight-games win at the Indonesia Open as India faltered elsewhere. Her return to the top 10 stood out on a day of early exits across singles and doubles.

Gautam Gambhir's coaching unit gets new hand for young spinners The BCCI has appointed Sairaj Bahutule as India's spin-bowling coach under Gautam Gambhir. He will mentor a young group of spinners as India try to rebuild their home Test edge.

Snoop Dogg's gospel: Why 19-year-old Andreeva always thanks herself at Slams Mirra Andreeva swept past Sorana Cirstea to reach another French Open semi-final and then explained her habit of thanking herself after big wins. The victory added a teenage Roland Garros record to her run.

PV Sindhu returns to top 10 in badminton rankings after nearly 3 yearsPV Sindhu returned to the world top 10 and beat Busanan Ongbamrungphan in straight games at the Indonesia Open. The result reinforced her recent revival before a fresh test against world No. 1 An Se-young.

India favourites for T20 World Cup: Ex-Indian captain makes bold claimWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Anjum Chopra has backed India to begin the T20 World Cup in England as one of the favourites. Her view rests on the squad's growing familiarity with conditions and the need for a more flexible batting order.

Messi vs Ronaldo? Football's biggest debate gets one last World CupFIFA World Cup 2026: As Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo prepare for what could be their final World Cup, football gets one last chance to celebrate the rivalry that defined an era.

Quarterfinals Highlights: Zverev moves into semis with clinical show vs JodarFrench Open 2026 Highlights: 7th seed Elena Svitolina has crashed out of the French Open after losing to 23-year-old Marta Kostyuk in the quarter-final of the tournament. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev beats Rafael Jodar in straight sets to move into his 5th Roland Garros semi-final.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland face tricky continental groupFIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland head a volatile Group B featuring Bosnia and Herzegovina, Canada and Qatar. The section will turn on veteran leaders, injury concerns and very little margin for error.

Man City's Rodri to decide future after World Cup as Real Madrid interest growsFIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri has parked questions over his Manchester City future until after Spain's World Cup campaign. His message to Spain is clear: work humbly, chase every ball, and ignore the noise.

After selection uproar, Auqib Nabi joins India's Test squad but with a twistIND vs AFG One-off Test: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Test squad sparked widespread debate, but the BCCI has now handed the Jammu and Kashmir pacer an unexpected opportunity, offering a twist to the selection story.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find himIPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Roland Garros Day 10 Order of Play: Zverev and next-gen stars take centre stageRoland Garros moves into a loaded day with Alexander Zverev, Elena Svitolina and Joao Fonseca all on the schedule. The draw also carries a Ukrainian derby, a late-night clash of disruptors and Indian interest in boys doubles.

Virat Kohli, RCB fired up as Mo Bobat calls for unprecedented IPL featIPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru ignited dreams of an unprecedented IPL three-peat after team director Mo Bobat delivered a stirring dressing-room rallying cry following their 2026 title victory over Gujarat Titans.

PV Sindhu wins Indonesia Open opener, potential An Se-young clash aheadPV Sindhu opened her Indonesia Open campaign with a straight-games win over Busanan Ongbamrungphan. The result sends her into the next round, where world No. 1 An Se-young is the likely challenge.

Franchise cricket's boom forces ICC to take action on jam-packed calendarThe ICC has set up a committee to examine how the surge of franchise leagues can coexist with the international calendar. The move reflects growing concern that bilateral cricket is losing ground as players and boards follow the T20 money.

Arnaldi outlasts Tiafoe in 5-hour epic to seal historic Italian trio in ParisRoland Garros: Matteo Arnaldi outlasted Frances Tiafoe in a five-set French Open epic to reach the quarter-finals. The comeback completed a rare Italian trio in the last eight and set up a meeting with Matteo Berrettini.

Sabalenka and Osaka unite after Roland Garros clash, want women in prime-time slotsRoland Garros 2026: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka formed a united front at French Open, demanding more prime-time visibility for women's tennis after their historic fourth-round night clash ended a three-year shutout.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget herRajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Sabalenka aces Michael Jackson's moonwalk after beating Osaka: VideoAryna Sabalenka beat Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-3 to reach the French Open quarter-finals before breaking into a moonwalk on Court Philippe-Chatrier. The celebratory flourish capped another commanding display from the World No. 1.

Contract crisis hits ISL as futures of nearly 150 players hang in the balanceNearly 150 ISL players became free agents after their contracts expired amid the AIFF-club stand-off over next season. The ongoing uncertainty has weakened players' bargaining power and hampered clubs' preparations for the upcoming campaign.

Berrettini avenges Jannik Sinner, knocks Cerundolo out in straight setsItalian Matteo Berrettini avenged his compatriot Jannik Sinner in the French Open after beating young Juan Manuel Cerundolo on Monday, June 1. Cerundolo's impressive run in the tournament was stopped in the fourth round.

World Cup: South Africa squad departs without assistant coach after US visa delaySouth Africa's World Cup squad departed for Mexico on Monday, 24 hours later than scheduled, after visa complications disrupted the team's travel plans and left assistant coach Helman Mkhalele stranded in Johannesburg.

Thomas Tuchel backs England to overcome heat and humidity at FIFA World CupEngland manager Thomas Tuchel has backed his side to cope with the heat and humidity expected at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, insisting the challenging conditions in North America will be an obstacle to overcome rather than an excuse for underperformance.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
